refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions to establish traceability.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsiveness sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator control with latency, situational awareness, and command fidelity sufficient to achieve productivity_target when operating in tele-operated mode.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without reducing achievable productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry (m³/h, ton/h, task completion status) to the Site Manager for monitoring and decision-making.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with responsive control systems and optimize operator interface (on-site and remote) to minimize task cycle time and maximize material throughput.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with configurable attachments and dual-mode control (on-site joystick + remote teleoperation) coupled with real-time productivity monitoring and adaptive task scheduling.

DP-C: Implement a productivity-optimized system architecture comprising: (1) a vehicle with high-speed hydraulics and low-latency control, (2) a teleoperation subsystem with <200ms round-trip latency and high-fidelity video/sensor feedback, (3) safety services that enforce constraints without blocking nominal task execution, and (4) telemetry aggregation for continuous productivity tracking.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a productivity-optimized system architecture comprising: (1) a vehicle with high-speed hydraulics and low-latency control, (2) a teleoperation subsystem with <200ms round-trip latency and high-fidelity video/sensor feedback, (3) safety services that enforce constraints without blocking nominal task execution, and (4) telemetry aggregation for continuous productivity tracking.

potential_sources: Vehicle hydraulic response time and teleoperation latency are coupled: slow vehicle response cannot be compensated by fast teleoperation, and vice versa. Mitigation: specify vehicle response time independently and teleoperation latency independently, then validate combined system latency.

Safety constraint enforcement and productivity are coupled: overly restrictive safety rules may prevent task execution. Mitigation: safety services must be designed to enforce policy without blocking nominal operations; safety rules must be validated against productivity targets.
Telemetry accuracy and productivity measurement are coupled: poor telemetry leads to inaccurate productivity reporting. Mitigation: telemetry subsystem must be validated for accuracy and latency.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is modular and addresses the four sub-FR domains (vehicle, teleoperation, safety, telemetry) as distinct but integrated concerns. Coupling is managed through explicit interface specifications (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and latency budgets.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic technology and operator skill levels. RISK: If target is unrealistic, no DP will succeed. MITIGATION: Validate target against historical data and pilot trials.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan does not impose constraints that fundamentally conflict with productivity (e.g., speed limits that make task infeasible). RISK: Safety and productivity may be in direct conflict. MITIGATION: Engage Safety Manager early to align constraints.

ASSUMPTION: Network infrastructure (for teleoperation) provides sufficient bandwidth and latency. RISK: Poor network conditions degrade teleoperation performance. MITIGATION: Specify network requirements and validate at deployment sites.

ASSUMPTION: Operator training and experience are adequate for both on-site and remote modes. RISK: Operator error may limit achievable productivity. MITIGATION: Include operator training and proficiency assessment in acceptance criteria.
RISK: High-speed hydraulics and low-latency teleoperation increase system complexity and cost. MITIGATION: Conduct trade studies to balance performance vs. cost.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound but should be refined to explicitly specify latency budgets (vehicle response <100ms, teleoperation round-trip <200ms, safety alert propagation <50ms) and define the telemetry aggregation logic (which metrics, sampling rate, accuracy requirements). This will enable clearer DP-to-IM mapping and testability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary that orchestrates the Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the system-level productivity measurement, telemetry aggregation, and coordination logic. The NGHE Solution receives Tasking commands from the Site Manager, coordinates execution across Vehicle and Teleoperation, enforces Safety Services constraints, and reports Telemetry back to stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager or Tele-operator sends task commands (dig, load, transport, dump, move to location) to NGHE Solution. Tasking includes task type, target location, and priority.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports real-time productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, task completion status, vehicle position, fuel level, cycle time) to Site Manager and Maintenance systems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services subsystem sends SafetyAlert to NGHE Solution when constraints are violated or risks detected. NGHE Solution may query Safety Services for constraint status before executing tasks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Teleoperation subsystem (for remote operator commands and video/sensor feedback) and with external systems (Site Manager dashboard, maintenance systems).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level integration boundary. It owns system-level productivity measurement, telemetry aggregation, and task orchestration. All four interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are naturally contained within NGHE Solution's scope. Diffusion risk is minimal: the only diffusion is the delegation of Vehicle control to the Vehicle module and remote operator control to Teleoperation module, which is intentional and well-defined.

Vehicle module owns the physical earthmoving machine and its control systems. However, productivity measurement, telemetry aggregation, and task orchestration logic are system-level concerns that should not be contained within Vehicle. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle would diffuse system-level logic across the vehicle hardware, making it difficult to evolve the system independently of hardware changes.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator control and video/sensor feedback. However, productivity measurement, safety constraint enforcement, and task orchestration are system-level concerns that should not be contained within Teleoperation. Assigning DP-C to Teleoperation would create a remote-operator-centric system that does not properly support on-site operation or system-level safety management.

Safety Services module owns constraint enforcement and risk detection. However, productivity measurement, vehicle control, and teleoperation are not safety concerns and should not be contained within Safety Services. Assigning DP-C to Safety Services would create a safety-centric system that does not properly address productivity or operational control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-C (productivity-optimized system architecture) is best contained within the NGHE Solution module because: (1) it is a system-level integration concern that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) it owns the productivity measurement and telemetry aggregation logic; (3) it manages the interfaces between subsystems (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link); (4) it is independent of specific vehicle hardware or teleoperation technology, allowing evolution of subsystems without changing system-level logic.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution may become a monolithic 'god object' that owns too much logic. MITIGATION: Clearly separate system-level orchestration (owned by NGHE Solution) from subsystem-level logic (owned by Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Use well-defined interfaces to enforce boundaries.

RISK: Productivity measurement logic may be complex and require frequent updates as acceptance criteria evolve. MITIGATION: Encapsulate productivity measurement in a dedicated module or service within NGHE Solution, with clear interfaces for metric definition and reporting.

RISK: Telemetry aggregation may require real-time processing and high throughput. MITIGATION: Specify telemetry latency and throughput requirements; consider dedicated telemetry processing infrastructure (e.g., time-series database, streaming pipeline).

RISK: Task orchestration logic may need to handle complex task sequences and error recovery. MITIGATION: Define task state machine and error handling logic explicitly; consider using a workflow engine or state machine framework.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution should be refined to explicitly define: (1) the task orchestration state machine (task states, transitions, error handling); (2) the productivity measurement algorithm (which metrics, sampling rate, aggregation logic); (3) the telemetry schema (which fields, data types, update frequency); (4) the interface contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link (message formats, latency requirements, error handling). This will enable clear delegation to subsystems and testable acceptance criteria.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 clearly states the productivity target and operational context (on-site and remote). DP-C directly addresses this by specifying a productivity-optimized architecture with four integrated subsystems (vehicle, teleoperation, safety, telemetry). The DP is traceable to the FR and provides a logical solution path. No fundamental mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module is implemented as a monolithic codebase, changes to productivity measurement logic may require changes to vehicle control or teleoperation logic, creating tight coupling. MITIGATION: Use modular architecture (e.g., microservices, plugin architecture) to isolate productivity measurement from subsystem control.

RISK: If telemetry aggregation is implemented in multiple places (vehicle, teleoperation, safety), metrics may be inconsistent or duplicated. MITIGATION: Centralize telemetry aggregation in NGHE Solution; subsystems report raw data via Telemetry interface.

RISK: If task orchestration logic is distributed across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, task sequencing and error recovery may be inconsistent. MITIGATION: Centralize task orchestration in NGHE Solution; subsystems execute tasks via Tasking interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-C is well-contained within NGHE Solution. The four subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and implicit Telemetry aggregation) are clearly separated, and their interactions are mediated by well-defined interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No significant diffusion detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Refined FR-NGHE-001 is verifiable through representative operations trials. Productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) can be measured directly. Compliance with safety plan and operational policies can be verified through audit and observation. Telemetry from NGHE Solution provides traceability for post-trial analysis.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: DP-C is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems respectively. Each sub-FR has a clear owner and measurable acceptance criteria.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify latency budgets and telemetry schema, as noted in the DP section.
Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary to define task orchestration state machine, productivity measurement algorithm, and interface contracts.
Establish clear ownership and interface contracts for each sub-FR (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to enable parallel development and testing.

Define acceptance criteria for each sub-FR in terms of measurable metrics (e.g., vehicle response time <100ms, teleoperation latency <200ms, safety alert propagation <50ms, productivity measurement accuracy ±5%).

decision.justification: Refined FR-NGHE-001 has been successfully decomposed into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4), each addressing a distinct subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry). DP-C provides a coherent solution architecture that integrates these subsystems. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for system-level orchestration and productivity measurement. The FR-DP-IM chain is sound and ready for further decomposition at the next level. Proceed to decompose each sub-FR independently.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsiveness sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator control with latency, situational awareness, and command fidelity sufficient to achieve productivity_target when operating in tele-operated mode.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without reducing achievable productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry (m³/h, ton/h, task completion status) to the Site Manager for monitoring and decision-making.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic technology and operator skill levels.
Worksite safety plan does not impose constraints that fundamentally conflict with productivity.
Network infrastructure provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for teleoperation.
Operator training and experience are adequate for both on-site and remote modes.
High-speed hydraulics and low-latency teleoperation are feasible within cost and complexity constraints.
open_risks: Productivity and safety constraints may be in direct conflict; early engagement with Safety Manager is critical.
Network latency and reliability are external dependencies that may limit teleoperation performance.
Operator skill and training are external dependencies that may limit achievable productivity.
Productivity_target may be unrealistic; validation against historical data and pilot trials is needed.
System complexity and cost may exceed project budget; trade studies are needed.
evidence_needed: Historical productivity data from similar equipment and operations to validate target feasibility.
Pilot trial results demonstrating productivity_target achievement in representative conditions.
Network latency and reliability measurements at deployment sites.
Operator proficiency assessment and training effectiveness data.
Cost and complexity trade study results.
Safety plan and operational policy documentation to identify constraints and conflicts.
Acceptance criteria and test plan for each sub-FR (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4).
